app.name: Anki Audio Quick Editor
settings.title: Audio Quick Editor Settings
settings.summary: Tune quick inline audio edits while keeping original Anki media untouched.
settings.tabs.label: Settings sections
settings.share_target.catbox.tooltip:
Permanent public upload while the Catbox service keeps it available.
Use this when you want a link that should last.
settings.share_target.litterbox.tooltip:
Temporary public upload that expires after about 3 days.
Use this for quick sharing when the link does not need to last.
settings.toolbar_visibility.title: Editor toolbar buttons
settings.toolbar_visibility.summary: Choose which quick editor buttons are shown beside audio fields.
graph.smoothness.raw.tooltip:
Least smoothed graph.
Shows the most detail, but the pitch line can have many sharp angles.
graph.smoothness.balanced.tooltip:
Good default for most speech.
Keeps visible detail while reducing small jagged changes.
graph.smoothness.smooth.tooltip:
Cleaner graph for reading overall intonation.
Hides more tiny bends and angles.
graph.smoothness.very_smooth.tooltip:
Smoothest graph.
Best when you only need the broad pitch movement, not every small corner.
settings.graph_connect_short_dropouts_ms: Connect holes in graph shorter than (ms)
graph.voice_range.note: Who is speaking on the recording? How low or high is the voice you hear?
graph.voice_range.bass.tooltip:
Lowest adult voices.
Example: deep male narration or a bass singer.
graph.voice_range.low.tooltip:
Lower voices.
Example: most adult male speech.
graph.voice_range.general.tooltip:
Middle-range voices.
Example: mixed speakers or any clip where you are not sure yet.
graph.voice_range.high.tooltip:
Higher voices.
Example: many adult female voices.
graph.voice_range.child.tooltip:
Highest voices.
Example: children, teens, or strong falsetto.
graph.recording_condition.note: How noisy or echoey is the recording around the speaker?
graph.recording_condition.auto.tooltip: Lets the analyzer guess the best setting for this clip.
graph.recording_condition.very_noisy.too:
For heavy background noise.
Example: street traffic, train noise, cafe chatter, or strong fan hiss.
graph.recording_condition.noisy.tooltip:
For noticeable noise.
Example: classroom recordings, office hum, or distant chatter.
graph.recording_condition.normal.tooltip:
For everyday recordings.
Example: phone speech in a mostly quiet room.
graph.recording_condition.clean.tooltip:
For clean close-mic audio.
Example: headset speech or a quiet study room.
graph.recording_condition.studio.tooltip:
For very clean recordings.
Example: treated room audio or a studio microphone.
graph.connect_dropouts.note: Bridges tiny missing gaps when the pitch line disappears for a moment.
graph.voice_lock.note: How much should the pitch line ignore quick jumps and stay on the same voice?
graph.voice_lock.loose.tooltip:
Follows quick jumps more easily.
Use this when you want more detail, even if the line looks jumpy.
graph.voice_lock.balanced.tooltip:
Keeps natural changes but filters some sudden jumps.
Good default for most speech.
graph.voice_lock.stable.tooltip:
Ignores more sudden jumps and keeps the line steadier.
Use this when the graph keeps hopping between harmonics.
settings.ffmpeg_path.help: Prefilled with a platform default. Change it if your ffmpeg binary lives elsewhere.
settings.dpdfnet_attn_limit_db.gentle.to:
Light cleanup.
Use when the recording is already clear and you want fewer artifacts.
settings.dpdfnet_attn_limit_db.normal.to:
Balanced cleanup.
Good default when noise is present but the voice is still clear.
settings.dpdfnet_attn_limit_db.aggressiv:
Strong cleanup.
Use when noise is distracting, but expect a higher chance of metallic artifacts.
settings.pause_detection_algorithm.deep_:
Uses the existing audio cleanup pipeline to find pauses.
Good default when you are already relying on DeepFilterNet.
settings.pause_detection_algorithm.siler:
Uses a speech detector trained to separate voice from silence.
Often faster and better for normal speech clips.
settings.denoise_algorithm.standard.tool:
General cleanup for everyday background noise.
Start here for most recordings.
settings.denoise_algorithm.rnnoise.toolt:
Light, fast speech denoise.
Use it for hiss, fan noise, or low-power machines.
settings.denoise_algorithm.voice_only.to:
Extracts the voice stem and can remove music or other instruments.
Use it for clips where speech is mixed with background audio.

editor.pitch_hum.mode.pitch_tier.title: Slightly more natural humming

editor.repeat.title: Repeat selected region, or the whole graph when no region is selected.
editor.repeat.label: Repeat
editor.help.title: Show editor help
editor.help.shift_drag: Shift-drag on the graph to select a region.
editor.help.play_repeat: Play uses the selected region when one is active; Repeat loops the selected region, or the full graph otherwise.
editor.help.delete_region: Delete Region removes only the selected region. Backspace does the same when the graph is focused.
editor.help.delete_region_or_rest: Delete Region removes the selected region; Delete the rest keeps only the selected region. Backspace deletes the selected region when the graph is focused.
editor.help.pitch_loudness: In the graph, grey is loudness and lines are pitch of the voice.
editor.help.share_desc: Share this file online. The link will be copied to the clipboard.
editor.help.convert_desc: Creates a new file in a different format.
editor.help.shorten_pauses_desc: Creates a new file with shorter pauses between sentences.
editor.help.pitch_hum_desc: Creates a new pitch-preserving hum file for intonation practice.
editor.help.denoise_desc: Creates a new file with noise or music reduced.
editor.help.slower_desc: Creates a new file with slower audio.
editor.help.faster_desc: Creates a new file with faster audio.
editor.help.volume_down_desc: Creates a new file with quieter audio.
editor.help.volume_up_desc: Creates a new file with louder audio.
editor.help.delete_region_desc: Creates a new file without the selected region.
editor.help.delete_region_or_rest_desc: Creates a new file that removes the selected region or keeps only that region.
editor.help.note: Every edit creates a new media file and updates the field to point at it. The original file remains in your media collection.
editor.status.region_read_failed: Could not read the selected region.
editor.status.graph_inactive: The selected graph is no longer the active field.
editor.status.graph_audio_mismatch: The selected graph no longer matches the current audio.
editor.status.delete_whole_clip: Cannot delete the whole audio clip.
editor.status.keep_whole_clip: Selection already covers the whole audio clip.
editor.status.selected_repeat_browser_au: Selected repeat playback needs browser audio.

batch.cancel_requested: Cancel requested; stopping after the current note.

batch.log.canceled: Canceled before starting the next note.

batch.summary.canceled: Canceled: {processed}/{total} notes processed, {written} written, {skipped} skipped, {failures} failures.

settings.reset_failed.title: Reset Failed
settings.reset_confirm.message: Reset all Audio Quick Editor settings to their defaults?
settings.log.missing: The Audio Quick Editor log file was not found.
diagnostics.links.title: Resources and feedback
diagnostics.links.summary: Open the website, community channels, or support forms.
batch.links.label: Batch resources
editor.status.split_defaults_invalid: Could not save those quick settings.
editor.status.current_field_audio_missin: No [sound:...] reference found in the current field.
editor.status.referenced_audio_missing: The referenced audio file was not found in Anki's media folder.
file_reveal.open_failed: Could not open the containing folder.
editor.processing_failed_note_unchanged: Audio processing failed. The note was not changed. ({error})
editor.split.description_speed: How much faster or slower to make the audio.
editor.split.description_volume: How much louder or quieter to make the audio.
editor.split.description_denoise: Removes noise and sometimes music from the audio. Different algorithms work better for different kinds of noise.
editor.split.description_share: Share this file online. The link will be copied to the clipboard.
editor.split.description_graph: To draw an accurate graph, we need to know some details about the speaker and surroundings.
editor.split.description_record_voice: Choose how many seconds to count down before recording starts.
editor.split.description_shorten_pauses: Attempts to remove pauses between sentences.
editor.split.description_pitch_hum: Turns speech into a pitch-preserving hum by removing consonants and vowel differences. Useful for practicing intonation.
editor.split.option.output_format.mp3.de: Most portable. Guaranteed to work in AnkiMobile and broadly supported everywhere.
editor.split.option.output_format.m4a.de: Small modern file with good quality, especially on Apple devices.
editor.split.option.output_format.wav.de: Uncompressed and large. Best for temporary editing, not daily review media.
editor.split.option.output_format.flac.d: Lossless and smaller than WAV. Good for archiving, but less portable than MP3.
editor.split.option.pause.gentle.descrip: Removes only longer silences, usually above about 700 ms. Lowest risk of clipping natural rhythm.
editor.split.option.pause.normal.descrip: Shortens common sentence gaps, usually above about 450 ms. Good default for review clips.
editor.split.option.pause.aggressive.des: Cuts shorter pauses too, often above about 250 ms. Can make speech feel rushed.
editor.split.option.pitch_hum.direct.des: Fast mode that follows detected pitch directly.
editor.split.option.pitch_hum.pitch_tier: Uses Praat PitchTier resynthesis when available. More controlled, but slower.
editor.play.description: Playback and repeat options for this field.
editor.status.original_audio_missing: The original audio file was not found in Anki's media folder.
settings.denoise_algorithm.dpdfnet.toolt:
Stronger modern denoise with an aggressiveness control.
Use it when Standard leaves too much noise.
editor.command.dpdfnet.label: DPDFNet
editor.command.dpdfnet.title: Create a new file cleaned with DPDFNet, Aggressiveness: {level}
